commands.repair_reason: Needs maintainer handling because the protected label and missing real behavior proof block cleanup/merge automation for this security-sensitive transport change.; Cleared: The diff is security-sensitive but appears to reduce cross-origin header-forwarding risk without adding dependencies, permissions, lifecycle hooks, or new secret storage. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a manual redirect-following fetch wrapper for streamable MCP HTTP transports, reuses the existing cross-origin header allowlist, and adds focused unit coverage for redirect header scrubbing.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence real reproduction was provided. Source inspection shows current main passes configured headers into the SDK streamable MCP transport without an OpenClaw-managed redirect wrapper, but the PR body says no after-fix real MCP redirect setup was run.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer handling because the protected label and missing real behavior proof block cleanup/merge automation for this security-sensitive transport change.
